subject: [PATCH 02/14] mm: rename uffd-wp PTE bit macros to uffd

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This patch renames the uffd-wp PTE bit macros to simply 'uffd' across various architectures, including x86 and arm64. The change is purely mechanical and does not alter any behavior. The rename reflects that the bit now tracks userfaultfd access rather than just write protection or NUMA hinting. This patch is part of a larger series of changes.

2026-05-04.patch_summary: This patch renames the uffd-wp PTE bit macros to simply 'uffd' in various architecture-specific header files, reflecting that the bit is used by userfaultfd for tracking access rather than just write protection or NUMA hinting. The rename is purely mechanical and does not change any behavior. This is done as a preparation for the uffd-wp PTE bit gaining a second consumer, which will use it to mark access-tracking PTEs.
